title: Q4_K_M superblock matmul — llama.cpp beachhead
goal: Implement the full ggml Q4_K_M format: 256-element superblocks with f16 scale/min, 8× 32-element sub-blocks with 6-bit packed scales+mins, and 4-bit packed weights. This is THE llama.cpp beachhead kernel — the quantization scheme used by mainstream LLM deployments (Llama 3 70B-Q4_K_M, Mistral, etc.). Proves AXIOM-Compute can match llama.cpp's Vulkan backend functionally.
scope_in: Q4_K_M superblock layout (144 bytes per 256-element superblock):
- 12 bytes: 8 × (6-bit scale + 6-bit min) packed (12 bytes = 96 bits = 8 × 12 bits)
6-bit scale/min unpacking: nibble-spread layout per ggml source. Scales in bytes[0..12], packed with 4-bit lo in bytes[0..4] and 2-bit hi in bytes[8..12] (and same for mins via byte overlap)
Dequant per element: value = d * scale[sub] * (nibble - 0) - m * min[sub] where scale/min are the unpacked 6-bit values [0..63]
Actually per ggml: value = d * (scale[sub]/63) * nibble - m * (min[sub]/63) — sub is the sub-block index (k / 32)
Wait — spec per llama.cpp GGML_TYPE_Q4_K: `y[l] = d * scale[is] * (q4 - 8) - dmin * m_[is]` where scales is lookup table of 4-byte packed 6-bit values. Re-read ggml source to pin exact formula.
Integration test: AT-1201 asserts bit-exact with ggml reference (within 1e-3 relative) on real GPU
CPU reference in common_helpers.rs: `q4km_dequant_matvec_cpu(q: &[u8], x: &[f32], n_superblocks: u32) -> f32`
Bench: dispatch_gpu_q4km_128 (128 superblocks = 18 KB weights, 32K elements) + dispatch_gpu_q4km_512 (128K elements)
scope_out: Cooperative matrix for the inner 4-bit dot products (M3; Q4_K_M uses f16 scales so coopmat math won't directly apply without dequant to f16 first)
Direct GGUF file loading (application layer, not compiler)
Perf parity with llama.cpp Vulkan (M3 after bandwidth work)
acceptance_criteria: q4km_dequant_matvec compiles to valid SPIR-V (in-process spirv-tools)
GPU dispatch matches CPU reference within 1e-3 relative tolerance on real GPU (NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell)
CPU reference exactly matches ggml's `dequantize_row_q4_K` semantics (bit-exact modulo f16 rounding)
New bench entries for dispatch_gpu_q4km_128 + dispatch_gpu_q4km_512 in baselines.json
Regression: 676 tests from prior milestones preserved
open_questions_for_architect: Exact 6-bit scale/min unpacking algorithm: ggml uses a cumbersome bit-spreading layout. Reference: llama.cpp ggml-quants.c `get_scale_min_k4()` function. Need to replicate in AXIOM-Compute kernel.
Dequant formula: `y[l] = d * scale[is] * q4 - dmin * m[is]` where q4 is the 4-bit unsigned nibble (no -8 offset unlike Q4_0). Verify.
Scales/mins are 6-bit unsigned [0..63], not signed. Verify semantics.
Byte indexing within the packed scale region: 8 sub-blocks × 6 bits = 48 bits = 12 bytes. 8 × 6-bit scales + 8 × 6-bit mins = 96 bits = 12 bytes total. Confirmed.
Do we need new AXIOM-Compute builtins? Probably not — existing ptr_read_u8_zext + band/lshr/bor cover it.
Workgroup size: one output element per invocation? Or sub-block-level parallelism? Start simple (one per output) for M2.6; tiling is M3.
